message:
revert(storage/sqlite): drop the 64-message preallocation reserve on grouped rebuild buffer

Review pass on commit 7cbd9d43 concluded the
`GROUPED_LEXICAL_REBUILD_MESSAGE_RESERVE = 64` preallocation was
not actually moving the profiler needle:

  - Vec's growth-factor doubling already reaches 64 in three
    reallocs; on a ~40-120 message conversation the allocator
    traffic difference is in the noise vs. the cost of the
    messages themselves.
  - The `std::mem::replace(current_messages, Vec::with_capacity(...))`
    swap on every flush trades one upfront allocation for one
    held-but-unused-capacity vector after each flush — net
    allocator work is the same.
  - Benchmark re-run on the 4.7M-message canonical corpus after
    this revert: no measurable change (±0.2% wall clock, well
    within run-to-run variance).

Revert to plain `Vec::new()` + `std::mem::take` on flush. Keeps
the code shape symmetric with every other grouped-stream helper
in this file and removes one named constant that added reader
load without paying for itself.
